questions.question: Design a deep learning network architecture for text classification, considering the following input features: word embeddings, positional information, and sentiment labels. Describe the layers and connections used in your architecture.
questions.hint: Consider the strengths and weaknesses of different architectures, such as CNN, LSTM, and Transformers.

questions.question: Implement a deep learning network using Python and the Keras library for text classification. Train the network on a dataset of labeled documents (e.g., spam vs. non-spam emails). Report the accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score on a test set.
questions.hint: Use a suitable preprocessing technique for text data, such as tokenization and stopword removal.

questions.question: Compare the performance of two different deep learning architectures for text classification: a CNN-based approach and a Transformer-based approach. Prepare a table to summarize the results, including metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score.
questions.hint: Consider the strengths and weaknesses of each architecture, as well as any limitations or assumptions made during implementation.
